The Department of Civil Engineering (CE) of IIT Jammu, one of six engineering departments, was started in the year 2017. The department offers four-year undergraduate courses, training the students in multiple core disciplines including Structural Analysis and Design, Traffic Engineering, Hydraulics, Construction Management, Environment Engineering, and Surveying, as well as exposure to interdisciplinary projects to enhance collaborative efforts and hone entrepreneurship skills. The department aligns its vision of being the collaborative innovation ambassador in Civil Engineering with the Institute’s mission objectives of passion for attempting to solve problems innovatively with a culture of mutual respect and creativity. #### Brief Info Get to know the iMaSS lab, click the link for details: https://sites.google.com/view/imass **About me** Dr. Ankit Kathuria is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering and Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu. Throughout his career, he has undertaken diverse roles, including his current position as Associate Dean for E-Governance System and his previous role as Head of the Department of Civil Engineering and Associate Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). He received the prestigious SERB-SIRE Fellowship in 2022, allowing him to contribute to significant research related to roundabout safety at the Future Mobility Group, Newcastle University, UK, for six months. Dr. Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. He obtained his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017. He received a Masters in Plannin

#### Research Interest

**Primary research interest**

c) Planning and design of dedicated powered two-wheeler lanes for urban and non-urban highways

d) Simulation-based AV's and human-driven vehicle interaction

e) Public transportation system design and planning with primary focus on Bus Rapid Transit Systems

f) Exploring adoption intentions of e-shared mobility alternatives

g) Traffic management using BT and Wifi sensors

**Secondary research interest**

h) Utilization of surface-modified aggregates in asphalt mixes

Civil EngineeringDeepak Yadav

### **Deepak Yadav**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Deepak Yadav is presently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu. He did PhD in Structural Engineering from IIT Delhi and BTech in Civil Engineering from IIT Roorkee. His doctoral research was focused on metallic-yielding dampers and he filed a patent on the developed damper as co-inventor. His research interests are seismic response control, retrofitting of structures, steel and aluminum structures, and earthquake-resistant design. Dr. Yadav also has industrial experience in the structural design of buildings in Africa, Middle East, and India regions. He has been a part of more than twenty projects as a structural engineer and peer reviewer.

### **Divyesh Varade**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Divyesh Varade is a budding expert on remote sensing and spatial analysis. Dr. Varade is working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics Engineering at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, since 2020. He received his PhD degree in Civil Engineering with specialization in Geoinformatics from IIT Kanpur in 2020 and his master’s degree in the International Program on Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology at the Technical University of Munich. He received the IEEE Senior Member title in November 2023, received the runner-up award for best PhD thesis by IEEE GRSS India in 2020. He has served several reputed journals as a referee with more than 100 listed reviews, and is also serving as the review editor for Frontiers in Remote Sensing for the mic

#### Professional Background

Dr. Divyesh M. Varade is an expert on remote sensing encompassing the domains of hyperspectral, thermal, and microwave remote sensing. Dr. Varade is presently working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. He is a research collaborator at the ATAL Centre for Artificial Intelligence at MJPRU-Bareilly and an advisor/consultant to Geo Climate Risk Solutions Pvt. Ltd. He received his Ph.D. degree in Geoinformatics from the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Kanpur in 2020. He was the IEEE GRSS India runner-up awardee for the best Ph.D. thesis category in 2020. In his doctoral research, he devised novel methods for the qualitative and quantitative characterization of snow using multi-sensor remote sensing data. His research integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis, and machine learning. He is also avidly interested in the hydrological

#### Brief Info

Dr. Prasun Halder has been serving the Civil Engineering Department of IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor since March 2022. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was a Research Fellow at IIT Delhi. Dr. Halder holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical Engineering from IIT Delhi.

His basic research interest lies in experimental and computational geomechanics with focus on foundation engineering, dam-foundation system on jointed rock, ground improvement, and soil dynamics. Currently, his research group at IIT Jammu is working on applied geomechanics domain to better understand the transportation geotechnics in hilly regions, application of geosynthetic-based solution for rock-fall hazard, tunnel & underground space, and cold-weather geotechnics.

#### Brief Info

Dr. Riya Bhowmik is an Assistant Professor of Civil Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. ​ She holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical and Geoenvironmental Engineering from IIT Delhi. Dr. Bhowmik has been with IIT Jammu since October 2020 and has also served as a Visiting Fellow at the University of Salerno, Italy.

Her research focuses on geosynthetics, ground improvement, and disaster mitigation. ​ Dr. Bhowmik publishes extensively in international journals and conferences, and has successfully secured several research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF) and Ministry of Textiles. ​She has been recognized as one of the 75 Influential Indian Women in Geotechnical Engineering and has received several awards, including the SERB National Post-Doctoral Fellowshi

# Page Title: Geotechnical Engineering

❮

❯

## Vision

Fostering disaster-resilient and sustainable infrastructure through specialized research in Himalayan terrains
and complex geo-materials.

Explore

## News and Updates

## Overview

The Geotechnical Engineering section at IIT Jammu bridges fundamental geomechanics with practical
engineering.
Strategically located in the Himalayan foothills, our program is uniquely positioned to address the complex
geotechnical challenges.

We specialize in the resilient design and performance of critical hill infrastructure, exploring the behavior
of
soil, rock, and geosynthetics to develop sustainable, disaster-resilient solutions for diverse geological and
climatic terrains.

## Why Geotechnical Engineering at IIT Jammu?

A living laboratory for Himalayan geotechnics

Merging GIS, Remote Sensing, and Machine Learning Approaches with core geotechnical engineering.

Focused on solving real-world infrastructure challenges in sensitive environments
